title: Garlicky Dry Uruguayan Strawberries Quesadilla
description: A twist on traditional quesadillas, this Uruguayan-inspired dish features dried strawberries, fresh garlic, and a hint of Mediterranean spices.

ingredients.name: 1 cup dried strawberries
ingredients.notes: Brined and dried from fresh strawberries

ingredients.name: 4 cloves garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for best flavor

ingredients.name: 1 cup shredded cheese (Monterey Jack)
ingredients.notes: A mild cheese perfect for quesadillas

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Add a fresh and herbaceous flavor

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality olive oil for best flavor

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: Season with salt to bring out the flavors

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.notes: A pinch of black pepper for added depth

instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the skillet and swirl to coat.
Place 1 tortilla in the skillet and sprinkle with shredded cheese, dried strawberries, minced garlic, chopped parsley, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the tortilla is crispy and the cheese is melted.
Flip the quesadilla and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the other side is also crispy and the cheese is melted.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling ingredients.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, and your favorite dipping sauce.
